channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Google urges Bitcoin network to upgrade security to defend against quantum computing threats
item.description: Google researchers have flagged that Bitcoin's $US1.4 trillion network requires urgent security upgrades to counter potential hacking from quantum computers. Their study suggests that fewer than 500,0...

item.title: DTI introduces MSME loan program with no collateral, offers up to P5M
item.description: The Department of Trade and Industry (DTI) in the Philippines has launched a new program aimed at supporting micro, small, and medium enterprises (MSMEs). Through this initiative, MSMEs can access loa...

item.description: A man is currently under investigation in Roodepoort, South Africa, after a viral video surfaced showing him allegedly collecting rent from beneficiaries of government-subsidised RDP (Reconstruction a...

item.title: Joseph Oketch Appointed Acting Director General at EPRA Following Kiptoo's Arrest
item.description: Joseph Oketch has been appointed the Acting Director General of the Energy and Petroleum Regulatory Authority (EPRA) after Daniel Kiptoo's arrest. Kiptoo's resignation comes in the wake of an ongoing ...

item.title: State pension age increases to 67 with updated payments
item.description: From Monday, the state pension age in the United Kingdom will begin rising from 66 to 67, affecting millions of people. The increase will occur gradually over the next two years. Those born between 6 ...

item.title: Experts Discuss Major Health Challenges Facing India on World Health Day
item.description: On the occasion of World Health Day, The Hindu compiled a series of expert-led articles drawing attention to key health challenges facing India today. The discussions span across multiple pressing iss...

item.title: Spiro rolls out 1,000 electric bikes in Ogun to support greener transport
item.description: Spiro, Africa’s leading electric mobility provider, has expanded its operations in Ogun State with the introduction of 1,000 electric bikes aimed at promoting sustainable transportation. The fleet was...

item.title: Sponsors withdraw from Wireless Festival following Kanye West headliner controversy
item.description: The upcoming 2026 edition of London’s Wireless Festival is facing major setbacks following the announcement that Kanye West would headline the event. The rapper’s inclusion has sparked widespread crit...

item.title: Phil Gould urges Dragons seniors to support young players
item.description: NRL commentator and former coach Phil Gould has expressed concern over the pressure being placed on young St George Illawarra Dragons players. Gould warned that a 'harrowing experience' could result i...

item.title: Addressing South Africa’s Social and Economic Crises Before They Escalate
item.description: Zwelinzima Vavi cautions that South Africa is at a critical point due to escalating unemployment, poverty, inequality, and corruption. With over 12 million people unemployed and most households surviv...

item.title: Hungary strengthens pipeline security amid false-flag accusations ahead of crucial election
item.description: Hungary has placed a critical gas pipeline under military protection after Serbia found explosives near it, prompting accusations of a potential false-flag operation. The timing of the discovery, just...

item.title: Comprehensive review links vaping to potential risk of oral and lung cancers
item.description: A new comprehensive review led by Professor Bernard Stewart from UNSW Sydney suggests that vaping, or the use of electronic cigarettes, is likely to cause oral and lung cancers. The research examined ...

item.title: Iran Declines Ceasefire Offer Amid Rising Regional Tensions
item.description: According to Iranian state media reports released early on Tuesday, the government of Iran rejected a recent ceasefire proposal aimed at halting ongoing hostilities in the Middle East. The rejection c...

item.title: DSWD provides support to Camarines Sur family after child reports barangay official’s abuses to President Marcos
item.description: The Department of Social Welfare and Development (DSWD) has extended support to the family of an 11-year-old girl from Tinambac, Camarines Sur, who wrote a letter to President Ferdinand Marcos Jr. see...

item.title: Potential Economic Impact of Escalating Tensions Between Iran and the US on Pakistan
item.description: Escalating tensions between Iran and the United States, particularly following recent incidents involving the downing of US drones and the threat of potential military action, are raising concerns abo...

item.title: Gbenga Daniel Reflects on His Rivalry with Amosun, Describes Him as a 'Rushed' Successor
item.description: Former Ogun State Governor, Senator Gbenga Daniel, has shared his thoughts on the political rivalry between him and his successor, Senator Ibikunle Amosun. Daniel, speaking at his 70th birthday celebr...

item.title: Trump signals shift towards ceasefire in Iran as public opinion turns against prolonged conflict
item.description: US President Donald Trump has indicated that the American public’s desire to bring troops back home from Iran is influencing his approach to the ongoing war. While he personally favours seizing Iran’s...

item.title: Justice Delivered After Six Years In Tamil Nadu Custodial Deaths Case
item.description: The NDTV news report details a harrowing incident of custodial deaths in Tamil Nadu that unfolded over six years ago, shedding light on the recent verdict that finally brought justice to the victims. ...

item.title: US Airman Rescued from Iran After High-Risk Mission Over Easter Weekend
